# Mr. IIoT

> Industrial IoT and systems engineering for manufacturers who want to grow
> without growing headcount. Mr. IIoT connects machines, integrates software, and
> automates the busywork — and gives owners real-time visibility into uptime,
> utilization, and cost — so the team you already have can do more. "The last mile
> of your factory network and the first mile of your information network." Audience:
> manufacturing business owners and operators facing growing pains who want to do
> more with fewer people. Founded and led by Chris Misztur (President & Founder),
> Greater Chicago Area, 25+ years in industrial IoT and Industry 4.0.

The site tells one story in five steps: connectivity → data collection →
integration → analytics → support.

## The five steps

- [Connectivity](index.html#connectivity): Two complementary paths. (1) SHARC — the universal IoT sensor adapter connects any digital or analog industrial sensor, from any manufacturer, to any network; powers the sensor and publishes over MQTT; works as a one-way data diode for secure/classified environments. (2) DIME (Data In Motion Enterprise, datainmotionenterprise.com) — the universal translator for industrial data; an edge connector speaks 50+ native industrial protocols (Fanuc, Yaskawa, Haas, Siemens S7, Modbus, OPC-UA, MTConnect, EtherNet/IP, and more), turning PLCs, robots, CNCs, databases, and brokers into one unified stream with sub-millisecond latency and zero custom code.
- [Data Collection](index.html#collection): Real-time capture of sensor readings and equipment event changes as open payloads (JSON over MQTT). The customer owns their machine data — no proprietary lock-in at the source.
- [Integration](index.html#integration): Two complementary tools. (1) DIME — DataOps engine that normalizes, transforms, and routes the unified stream to 16+ destinations (OPC-UA, SparkplugB, Splunk, MongoDB, Websocket, and more); standards-based (MTConnect, OPC-UA, SparkplugB). (2) i3X (Industrial Information Interoperability Exchange, i3x.net) — turns disparate sources (SQL databases, MTConnect, OPC-UA, CSV/Excel) into standardized, queryable i3X-compliant REST APIs with semantic models and no custom code; auto schema discovery, one-click mapping, multi-server aggregation, AI-powered search, and MCP integration.
- [Analytics](index.html#analytics): Real-time monitoring, custom dashboards, predictive maintenance, and advanced reporting. Scalable machine data collection with no vendor lock-in.
- [Support](index.html#support): Tracebook (tracebook.ai) — AI tech support for machine OEMs. Turns manuals, schematics, and resolved tickets into a searchable, cited knowledge base that answers diagnostic questions in seconds. White-label under the OEM's brand, per-machine-serial knowledge bases, multi-modal input (text/photo/video), closed-loop ticket escalation.

## Problems we solve (and how)

Every Mr. IIoT engagement follows one pattern: start with a manufacturer's pain, end with a system that removes it. Common problems — phrased the way owners and operators actually describe them — and how we solve them:

- Problem: "We're growing faster than we can hire" — demand is up, but skilled people are hard to find and take months to ramp. Solution: automate the busywork and add real-time visibility so the team you already have produces more — grow output without growing headcount. ([Home](index.html), [Order Processing](case-order-processing.html))
- Problem: "My team burns hours on manual data entry, re-keying the same orders across quoting, ERP, and shipping." Solution: integrate the systems with DIME and i3X and automate the hand-offs so data flows once, automatically; automate EDI order/invoice/ASN workflows seven days a week. ([i3X](i3x.html), [DIME](dime.html), [Order Processing](case-order-processing.html))
- Problem: "Our systems don't talk — ERP, machines, spreadsheets, email — so our people are the glue." Solution: DIME normalizes and routes machine and business data into one coherent stream; i3X exposes legacy and heterogeneous sources as standardized, queryable REST APIs. ([DIME](dime.html), [i3X](i3x.html), [Middleware Solutions](case-middleware-solutions.html))
- Problem: "Getting one machine online costs a five-figure OEM quote, and half our assets speak protocols nothing else understands." Solution: SHARC connects any digital or analog sensor for a fraction of an OEM quote; DIME speaks 50+ native industrial protocols (Fanuc, Yaskawa, Haas, Siemens S7, Modbus, OPC-UA, MTConnect, EtherNet/IP) with zero custom code. ([SHARC](sharc.html), [DIME](dime.html), [Aerospace IT/OT](case-aerospace-it-ot.html), [Digital Transformation](case-digital-transformation.html))
- Problem: "I can't see where we're losing time or money — utilization, downtime, and cost are a guess until month-end." Solution: real-time machine monitoring, OEE, and custom dashboards (e.g., Grafana) built from live telemetry, so decisions run on facts. ([Analytics](index.html#analytics), [Machine Monitoring](case-machine-monitoring.html))
- Problem: "Downtime catches us off guard — maintenance is reactive, and by the time it shows on a report the line has already stopped." Solution: real-time alerting and predictive maintenance on live machine data, surfacing problems before they stop the line. ([Analytics](index.html#analytics))
- Problem: "Our machine data is trapped in a black box we don't control, and we can't switch tools without breaking everything." Solution: open payloads (JSON over MQTT), standards-based delivery (MTConnect, OPC-UA, SparkplugB), and customer-owned data with no proprietary lock-in at the source. ([Data Collection](index.html#collection))
- Problem: "We need FDA-compliant product traceability and serial-level tracking across multiple sites." Solution: a custom Serial Shipping Container Code (SSCC) system integrated with ERP and WMS, plus SHARC sensors and print-and-apply automation. ([Compliance & Traceability](case-compliance-traceability.html))
- Problem: "Every new customer adds more EDI, labels, and paperwork instead of leverage." Solution: automate EDI (e.g., SPS Commerce ↔ ERP) and deploy scan-print-apply labeling middleware that bridges printers, scanners, and client networks. ([Order Processing](case-order-processing.html), [Middleware Solutions](case-middleware-solutions.html))
- Problem: "Our engineers are stuck answering the same customer questions instead of building product — and the answer is buried in a 400-page manual when the call comes at 2 a.m." Solution: Tracebook, white-labeled AI tech support trained on the OEM's manuals, schematics, and resolved tickets, returning cited answers in seconds, scoped per machine serial, with closed-loop escalation. ([Tracebook](tracebook.html), [Free Your Engineers](case-tracebook-support.html))

## Who it's for (industries & use cases)

Mr. IIoT serves manufacturers and machine OEMs facing growing pains — across aerospace, packaging, food & baking, fluid systems, contract machining and CNC shops, and capital-equipment builders. Typical use cases: industrial IoT (IIoT) and Industry 4.0 / digital transformation; IT/OT connectivity and convergence; machine monitoring, OEE, and predictive maintenance; SCADA/MES/historian and ERP integration; EDI and order-processing automation; FDA traceability and SSCC labeling; custom middleware and scan-print-apply labeling; and AI-powered after-sale technical support. Relevant technologies and standards include MQTT, SparkplugB, MTConnect, OPC-UA/OPC-DA, Modbus/TCP, EtherNet/IP, Siemens S7, Beckhoff ADS, and controllers from Fanuc, Yaskawa, Haas, and Brother — with edge collection, JSON data, and no vendor lock-in.

## Pages

- [Home](index.html): The four-step story, customer pain points, and proof.
- [SHARC](sharc.html): Product deep dive — features, the "measure anything" sensor gallery (throughput, pressure, temperature, current, vibration, flow, level, PLC tap, and more), open-platform ingestion (JSON/MQTT), and security (one-way data diode).
- [DIME](dime.html): Product page for Data In Motion Enterprise — 50+ protocols, sub-millisecond latency, zero custom code, the Connector/Horizons/Zenith three-tier architecture, and AI-from-documentation setup. Links to datainmotionenterprise.com.
- [i3X](i3x.html): Product page for the Industrial Information Interoperability Exchange — standardized queryable REST APIs, semantic models, and the suite (i3xdb, i3xmt, i3xcsv, i3xopc, i3xview, i3xdash, i3xrag, i3X Explorer). Links to i3x.net.
- [Tracebook](tracebook.html): Product page for AI tech support for machine OEMs — cited answers, multi-modal input, per-machine knowledge bases, white-label, closed-loop escalation. Links to tracebook.ai.
- [Case Studies](case-studies.html): Summary listing — seven real, named engagements, each linking to a full write-up:
  - [Aerospace IT/OT Connectivity](case-aerospace-it-ot.html): connected hundreds of assets to a worldwide aerospace firm's OT network.
  - [Digital Transformation](case-digital-transformation.html): open-source FANUC data collector for a global fluid-systems manufacturer.
  - [Machine Monitoring](case-machine-monitoring.html): Ox Box — SHARC sensors + Grafana for real-time uptime/productivity (and COVID safety scanners).
  - [Compliance & Traceability](case-compliance-traceability.html): nationwide bakery — custom SSCC system + SHARC for FDA traceability.
  - [Middleware Solutions](case-middleware-solutions.html): Chicago Coding Systems — custom middleware for real-time scan-print-apply labeling.
  - [Order Processing](case-order-processing.html): Tempco — automated EDI between SPS Commerce and ERP.
  - [Free Your Engineers](case-tracebook-support.html): Royal Master Grinders — Tracebook AI support answers repetitive customer questions so engineers get back to product work.
- [Media](media.html): Video demos and setups (SHARC, DIME, Fanuc-Driver with MTConnect/SparkplugB/InfluxDB).
- [About](about.html): Mission, services, product, and founder Chris Misztur.

## Products

- SHARC: Universal IoT sensor adapter. Connects any sensor to any network; publishes telemetry over MQTT/JSON. (Connectivity)
- DIME (datainmotionenterprise.com): Universal translator for industrial data; 50+ protocols in, normalized and routed anywhere. (Connectivity + Integration)
- i3X (i3x.net): Industrial Information Interoperability Exchange; turns any data source into standardized, queryable REST APIs with semantic models. (Integration)
- Tracebook (tracebook.ai): AI tech support for machine OEMs; transforms manuals, schematics, and resolved tickets into a searchable knowledge base that answers diagnostic questions with cited sources. White-label, per-machine knowledge bases, sub-45-second responses. (After-sale support)
